_doc: Machine-readable release baselines for the M4 milestone (alignment methodology resolution + BRR encoder noise-floor investigation + conditional pre-emphasis presets). Scaffolded at M4.0 with prelude contract entries; M4.1+ populates with measurement and encoder-quality baselines as the research-spike sub-passes land. Inherits M3 by reference (which inherits M2). Three classifications mirror M3: identity_gated (any drift = regression; ships with include_str! + serde-parse + assert_eq! test per M2.8.1 rule), behavior_gated (numeric or policy contracts), documentary_snapshot (informational; expected to shift on declared milestones).

behavior_gated.name: M4_RELIABLE_ALIGNMENT_CRITERIA
behavior_gated.rule: M4 characterization is RELIABLE only if all four criteria hold for every monotonicity-anchor signal per SPEC §10.9 amendment: (1) zcr_ratio in [0.9, 1.1]; (2) normalized_correlation >= 0.90; (3) alignment_best_offset < alignment_search_limit; (4) peak_abs_error_after_gain_normalization materially lower than peak_abs_error_oracle_vs_raw. Correlation threshold of 0.90 locked at M4.0 without PM-review change.

behavior_gated.name: M4_ALIGNMENT_SEARCH_LIMIT
behavior_gated.rule: alignment_search_limit equals max_i(cycle_len_samples_i) across the signal set. For m3_5_canonical with cycles {64, 128, 256}, this yields 256. M3.5.1's max_offset = 32 is superseded; rationale in SPEC §24.

behavior_gated.name: M4_BRR_NOISE_FLOOR_METRICS
behavior_gated.rule: Four BRR encoder noise-floor metrics locked at M4.0 per SPEC §10.10. Compared against the rotated source PCM (SPEC §10.7), not the pre-rotation original. Formulas fixture-pinned in core/tests/brr_noise_floor_metric.rs (14 tests). Wired through the encoder path at M4.3.

behavior_gated.name: M4_ENCODER_SPIKE_EXIT_CRITERION
behavior_gated.rule: M4.4 encoder-improvement spike ships a production change ONLY IF at least one canonical/edge fixture improves rms_raw_vs_source OR peak_abs_raw_vs_source by >= 10% AND no fixture's loop_click_abs worsens AND no M2 behavioral gate regresses AND encode runtime stays within 2x the M3.3 phase-rotation baseline. A negative finding (BRR encoder near local optimum under current constraints) is an acceptable close.

behavior_gated.name: M4_METHODOLOGY_REPAIR_BUDGET
behavior_gated.rule: M4.1 alignment fix + M4.2 characterization re-run + at most one M4.2.1 correction iteration. If alignment remains anomalous after this budget (any monotonicity-anchor signal still violates the four reliable-alignment criteria), characterization is declared unreliable and pre-emphasis defers to M5+. M4 proceeds to M4.3 BRR noise-floor work without expecting characterization-driven decisions.

behavior_gated.name: M4_PRE_EMPHASIS_PIPELINE_ORDER
behavior_gated.rule: When pre-emphasis presets ship at M4.5 (conditional), the encoder pipeline runs in this exact order. Atom PCM render output is identity-gated (SPEC §16.9) and refers to the pre-filter render; pre-emphasis operates on a transient encoder input; phase rotation operates on the filtered PCM; BRR encodes the rotated-filtered PCM. The SPEC §10.7 lex objective and SPEC §10.10 noise-floor metrics compare against the rotated-filtered source when pre-emphasis is active, the rotated unfiltered source otherwise.

documentary_snapshot.name: M4_2_GAUSSIAN_ALIGNMENT_BEST_OFFSET_SINE_CYCLE_64
documentary_snapshot._note: M4.1 alignment search-range expansion (max_offset = sine_cycle_64's cycle_len_samples) produced this offset. M3.5.1 capped at 32 could not resolve offsets > 32. Documentary — shifts if alignment algorithm changes.

documentary_snapshot.name: M4_2_GAUSSIAN_NORMALIZED_CORRELATION_SINE_CYCLE_64
documentary_snapshot._note: Pearson correlation between aligned raw BRR decode and aligned mono oracle render. M4.0 reliable-alignment threshold = 0.90; values below trigger methodology_review.

documentary_snapshot.name: M4_2_GAUSSIAN_ZCR_RATIO_SINE_CYCLE_64
documentary_snapshot._note: zcr_oracle / zcr_raw. M4.0 reliable-alignment sanity band = [0.9, 1.1]. M4.2 investigation revealed the ~2x doubling on low/mid signals is intrinsic to SPC playback at non-native pitch + BRR + gaussian, not a methodology bug.

documentary_snapshot.name: M4_2_GAUSSIAN_GAIN_DELTA_DB_SINE_CYCLE_64
documentary_snapshot._note: M3.5-form gain delta (raw-window form). M4.2 values are essentially unchanged from M3.5.1 since alignment fix doesn't move RMS by much.

documentary_snapshot.name: M4_2_GAUSSIAN_GAIN_DELTA_DB_ALIGNED_SINE_CYCLE_64
documentary_snapshot._note: M3.5.1-form gain delta (aligned-window form per consultant M3.5 audit #3). Recommended reference if a future pass designs pre-emphasis presets.

documentary_snapshot.name: M4_2_GAUSSIAN_PEAK_ABS_ERROR_ORACLE_VS_RAW_SINE_CYCLE_64
documentary_snapshot._note: Max-abs delta between aligned oracle and tiled raw decode.

documentary_snapshot.name: M4_2_GAUSSIAN_PEAK_ABS_ERROR_AFTER_GAIN_NORMALIZATION_SINE_CYCLE_64
documentary_snapshot._note: Max-abs delta after rescaling oracle to match raw RMS. If close to peak_abs_error_oracle_vs_raw -> shape divergence, not gain divergence. M4.2 found this is the case for all 9 signals -> waveform-shape differs between raw decode (1:1 tile) and oracle (DSP-played at non-native pitch).

documentary_snapshot.name: M4_2_GAUSSIAN_ALIGNMENT_VALIDITY_ALL_PASS_SINE_CYCLE_64
documentary_snapshot._note: AND of the four SPEC §10.9 reliable-alignment criteria for this signal. M4.2 finding: 0/9 signals all-pass; criterion 4 (gain_separator_ok) fails universally.

documentary_snapshot.name: M4_2_CHARACTERIZATION_SUMMARY
documentary_snapshot._note: M4.2 outcome: alignment search-range expansion (M4.1) produced new offset values but did not clear the methodology anomaly. Phase C investigation identified the cause as intrinsic to the SPC playback path (BRR + non-native pitch + gaussian DSP), not a fixable methodology bug. Per consultant M4 plan #16 (one M4.2.1 correction budget), no correction iteration was burned. Pre-emphasis preset implementation (M4.5) defers permanently to M5+; M4 proceeds to M4.3 BRR noise-floor measurement without expecting characterization-driven decisions.

documentary_snapshot.name: M4_2_PHASE_C_ZCR_DOUBLING_ROOT_CAUSE
documentary_snapshot._note: Phase C tested three hypotheses for the M3.5.1/M4.1 zcr_ratio doubling on low/mid signals: (1) oracle sample-rate doubling - RULED OUT (raw_pcm_length == oracle_pcm_length == 16000 mono samples per run); (2) oracle output shape - ROOT CAUSE FOUND (oracle output for sine_cycle_128 has ~568 leading silent samples then alternating-amplitude plateau regions connected by discontinuous jumps; each plateau-to-plateau jump adds 2 zero crossings beyond a clean sine, producing the 2x ZCR ratio); (3) oracle invocation review - RULED OUT (oracle configured at sample_rate_hz=32000, channels=2, frames=16000 per oracle-side report). Cause: SPC plays the 128-sample atom at MIDI 60 = 261.63 Hz; atom's native rate at cycle_len 128 = 33489 Hz; pitch register fractionally steps through input samples per output sample, and combined with BRR quantization + gaussian interpolation produces the alternating-amplitude shape. Not a methodology bug; the characterization is fundamentally measuring 'raw 1:1 tile' vs 'SPC pitch-shift + gaussian playback'.

documentary_snapshot.name: M4_4_SPIKE_OUTCOME
documentary_snapshot._note: M4.4 outcome: SKIP. Two of four SPEC §24.1 exit criteria failed. Criterion 1 (>= 10% improvement on rms or peak for at least one fixture): FAILS — best RMS improvement was 2.41% on 64_SINE (with peak +1.4%); best peak improvement was 0.16% on CYCLE_256_CANONICAL_SINE. Criterion 4 (runtime <= 2x M3.3): FAILS at 7.73x. Criteria 2 (no loop_click worsening) and 3 (no M2 regression, trivially via no-swap) pass. Spike implementation stays in core::brr_encoder feature-flagged for M5+ reference. The high-noise cluster's peak=18431 ceiling is arithmetically structural (32767 - 14336 = max i16 source minus max BRR-decoded value at shift=12); no cross-block trajectory search can fix it. Acceptable close per consultant M4 plan #17.

documentary_snapshot.name: M4_4_BRR_NEAR_LOCAL_OPTIMUM_FINDING
documentary_snapshot._note: The high-noise atom-fixture cluster's peak_abs_raw_vs_source = 18431 is arithmetically structural, not a search-greediness artifact. Max BRR-decoded value at shift = 12 (highest non-degenerate shift; shift 13+ triggers the 'negative -> -2048, positive -> 0' special-case path that's unusable for music) is 7 * 4096 / 2 = 14336. For source samples > 14336 (common in normalize=false / max-amp fixtures), the encoder literally cannot match; min |source - decoded| at the peak sample is source - 14336, which for source = i16::MAX gives 32767 - 14336 = 18431. No cross-block beam search can find trajectories around this. Future encoder improvements would need to either (a) operate on the source PCM before encoding (e.g. attenuation, which §16.9 forbids without amplitude field change; or pre-emphasis, M4.5 permanently deferred), or (b) extend the BRR spec into shift 13+ usage, which would alter the playback contract and require Mesen2 / snes_spc oracle validation.
